# Alternate & feminine headword candidates (item F)

The agent-doable slice of [PRINT_READINESS.md](PRINT_READINESS.md) item **F**: machine-
generated *candidate* lists for the editor to rule on. The **policy** (headword a
feminine separately / fold under the masculine with `mf(ā/ī)` / merge variants with a
"see X" cross-reference) is human — these are the rows to decide on. Generated by
[`alternate_headwords.py`](alternate_headwords.py) from the 2026 key1 sets; data in
[`f_candidates/`](f_candidates/) (SLP1 + IAST columns).

## MW (the print spine)

| candidate set | count | file | what it is |
|---|--:|---|---|
| feminine ↔ masculine pairs | **5,036** | `MW_fem_masc.tsv` | a feminine stem **and** its masculine base both headworded (`-ā↔-a`, `-inī↔-in`, `-ī↔-in`) — the decide-fold-or-keep set |
| orphan feminines | 22,298 | `MW_orphan_fem.tsv` | `-ā/-ī/-inī` stems with **no** masculine base headworded — mostly inherently-feminine nouns, not "missing masculines" |
| variant-spelling pairs | 1,217 | `MW_variants.tsv` | both forms headworded, differing by `b~v`, `ś~ṣ`, or geminate~single — candidate "see X" merges |
| multi-`<k2>` alternate groups | 0 | `MW_multi_k2.tsv` | one entry listing several alternate forms — **negligible** (MW key2 is one form per entry) |

`SKD` is also generated (`f_candidates/SKD_*`) as a union-case sample (258 / 9,540 / 555 / 0).

## How to read — and the caveats

- **These are candidates, not decisions.** The fem↔masc pairing is *morphological-shape*
  based, so it includes semantic non-pairs the editor must drop — e.g. the very first row
  pairs `A` (ā) with `a` (a): distinct vowels/lemmas, not a feminine-of. Treat the TSV as a
  worklist to filter, not a merge to apply.
- **Variant pairs** likewise mix true orthographic variants (`āśāḍha ~ āṣāḍha`, `ābadh ~
  āvadh`) with coincidental homographs; the editor confirms each.
- **Orphan feminines** are large because Sanskrit has many inherent `-ā/-ī` nouns; the
  actionable subset is small — included for completeness, not as a to-merge list.
- **For the MW/PWG print spine the real work is the 5,036 pairs + 1,217 variants**; the
  multi-`<k2>` alternate-form problem is effectively absent. It grows for a cross-dict
  **union** (item E), where the same lemma's masc/fem/variant differ across dictionaries.

Run for any dictionary: `python alternate_headwords.py <DICT>` (default `MW`).
